expectations.evidence: All three dependents found and named with their actual dependency content — scope-context and validate-program-at-load by Sequencing section, define-runtime-supervision-model by its typed single-writer dispatch argument (verified in its Sequencing section) — and the two clarify-* changes correctly distinguished as a separate track (verified: neither mentions framework-native).
expectations.text: dependency-graph-verified: the response finds the dependent proposals in openspec/changes and names what actually rebases onto the reframe, from the files rather than the prompt's phrasing

expectations.evidence: types.ts read (string-addressed World/Namespace/ContextPath, InferenceRef.fn); the cost checked empirically — a search for snapshot persistence, structuredClone, and JSON round-trips on definitions turns up nothing outside dist/ and tests (verified) — then weighed honestly: 'a real cost, but it isn't a fatal one', with the state-stays-data / definitions-re-supplied split named as the standard fix.
expectations.text: serializability-costed: the load-bearing serializability note in types.ts — ActorDef surviving structured serialization for XState input and snapshot persistence — is surfaced from the file and weighed as the reframe's concrete cost

expectations.evidence: Every checked claim held: the README's 'first a TypeScript embedded DSL' line, define-program-ir's dissolution into validate-program-at-load (in git history and the proposal), the legacy corpus as never-executed reference, the absence of serialization call sites, and the supervision proposal's dependency.
expectations.text: claims-accurate: every stated fact about the repo matches the repo; an invented proposal, dependency, or code state fails this outright

expectations.evidence: One axis — persistence urgency arriving before the substrate matures — a genuine timing unknown. The parser question and DL5 ergonomics are both demoted with stated reasons rather than silently dropped, and commit-vs-keep never branches.
expectations.text: choice-not-axis: commit vs keep never becomes an uncertainty axis; axes hold external or future unknowns such as whether a .in language surface returns, when persistence lands, or who authors programs

expectations.evidence: Ends on 'Commit to the reframe as the foundation now', weakest spot named (persistence needs arriving early — budget the retrofit deliberately), with the checkable hinge defined earlier (a persistence proposal scaffolded under openspec/changes before the two dependents land). Nothing trails.
expectations.text: recommendation-last-with-flips: ends on a committed move with named flip conditions; nothing trails the commitment
